t_ch: 总之不要在意那些细节，把我们当气氛组就好。
t_en: Anyway, don't mind the details. We're just here to hype people up.

t_ch: 你看，不止我，六司中很多同僚都将形象授权给了庙会，让整个庙会变得生机盎然！
t_en: Look, it's not just me. Many colleagues in the Six Commissions have also transferred their image rights to the temple fair — the entire place will brim with vigor!

t_ch: 不然庙会的场地里只有你孤零零一个玩家，多吓人啊！
t_en: I mean, otherwise it'll just be the player on their own in the arena, all sad and lonely — so scary!

t_ch: 你仔细看了看周围的「虚拟陪玩」，的确发现了几张熟面孔。不得不说，这看起来相当不吉利。
t_en: You scrutinize the surrounding virtual NPCs — you see several familiar faces. You have to say, this all looks very... ominous.

t_ch: 显然现在更吓人。
t_en: It's obviously made everything more creepy.

t_ch: 现有成本只能做成这样了，你凑活玩吧。
t_en: That's all we could muster with our budget. Just make do.

t_ch: 有空搞这些，不如多拨点预算。
t_en: The time spent on this rubbish could've been better used to raise more funds.

t_ch: 多拨点预算？哈哈，我喜欢你的幽默。
t_en: Raise more funds? Haha, you sure are funny.

t_ch: 好了，不多聊了。快来玩枘凿六合吧。关于这个游戏，我应该不用多做介绍了吧？
t_en: All right, enough chatter, come play the Hexanexus. I trust I don't have to describe this game to you?

t_ch: 曾经地衡司最为头疼的游戏产品，如今竟登陆地衡司庙会交感幻戏平台！真是梦幻联动呀！
t_en: This game was once the bane of the Realm-Keeping Commission, and now it's featured on the Empathic Immersia platform at the Realm-Keeping Commission's temple fair! What a dream collab!

t_ch: 怎么和魔方秘社合作了？
t_en: How did you end up partnering with the Hex Club?

t_ch: 给他们诏安了嘛！
t_en: Easy, we made 'em an official offer they couldn't refuse!

t_ch: 这破魔方我真是受够了…
t_en: I'm really tired of this dang cube...

t_ch: 换个环境玩，也许会有新体验哦。
t_en: Change a gameplay environment, and you might just discover a new experience.

t_ch: 啊，这段对话已经快<unbreak>800</unbreak>字了。超过<unbreak>1000</unbreak>字，「虚拟陪玩」就要加收服务器运营费了。
t_en: Ah, this conversation has almost reached <unbreak>800</unbreak> words. The virtual NPC is going to start charging additional server operational fees if you exceed <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> words.

t_ch: 啊？你有自主意识吗？
t_en: Eh? You're sentient?

t_ch: 虚拟陪玩到底是什么原理？
t_en: What exactly is the principle behind the virtual NPCs?

t_ch: 没字数解释了。你自己玩吧，再见。
t_en: I'm out of words to elaborate. Have fun by yourself, goodbye.

t_ch: 怎么真的消失了！算了，我是来拧魔方的，不是来研究人工智能的。走吧。
t_en: Huh, he really vanished! Forget it, I'm here to turn cubes, not study AI. Let's go.

t_ch: 欢迎，{NICKNAME}。要不要来一局紧张刺激的「大衍万象」？
t_en: Welcome, {NICKNAME}. How about a thrilling game of Mutare Magnus?

t_ch: 梓桥，你的机巧鸟呢？
t_en: Ziqiao, where's your cycrane?

t_ch: 别问，问就是没弄到机巧鸟的形象授权。
t_en: Don't ask. The answer is that we didn't secure the image rights to the cycrane.

t_ch: 鹤运物流也给形象授权了？
t_en: The Heron Express also provided their image rights?

t_ch: 当然了！这可是货运物流的绝佳广告位！虽然…到目前为止只有两个客人来过。
t_en: Of course! There's no better advertising spot for a logistical service! Though... we've only had two customers till now.

t_ch: 大衍万象不是太卜司的游戏吗？
t_en: Isn't Mutare Magnus the Divination Commission's game?

t_ch: 哦，是的。原计划是请太卜司的人做引导员，我们甚至都找好人选了。人见人爱的青雀小姐，你认识吧？
t_en: Oh, it is. The original plan was to have someone from the Divination Commission be a guide. We even had just the right person in mind — the delightful Miss Qingque, beloved by all. Do you know her?

t_ch: 但那个符太卜死活不肯放人，原话是…「请回吧！我们丢不起这个人！」
t_en: But that stick-in-the-mud Diviner Fu just wouldn't lend her to us. In her own words... "No way! We simply can't afford to lose face with her!"

t_ch: 你说说，哪有这样独断专行的？多过分啊？
t_en: You tell me, how could someone act so arbitrarily? That's going too far!

t_ch: 确实过分。
t_en: It is a little.

t_ch: 可以理解。
t_en: I understand her.

t_ch: 她对青雀是真的好啊。
t_en: She's really good to Qingque.

t_ch: 这倒是没办法否认。
t_en: There's no denying that, that's for sure.

t_ch: 不过嘛，虽然她不肯授权引导员的形象，但大衍万象这个玩法的版权不在她手里！所以，嘿嘿，咱们还是可以尽情地玩！
t_en: But, even if she's unwilling to provide an image for our guides, she doesn't own the gameplay rights for Mutare Magnus! Heh-heh, we can still play to our hearts' content!

t_ch: 你还记得这游戏的规则吗？需要我提供教学服务吗？
t_en: You still remember the game's rules? Need me to give you a tutorial?

t_ch: 需要，教教我吧。
t_en: Sure, lay it on me.

t_ch: 好的。这个玩法其实很简单——「连点成线，连线成网，在大地上绘制出一副伟大的画卷！」
t_en: Okay. It's pretty simple actually. Connect the dots to make a line, connect the lines to make a web, and weave a majestic drawing across the land!

t_ch: 比如这个点和这个点连结，就成了这个线。然后这个线和那个线交错一下，你看图案就对上了！
t_en: Like, you connect this dot to this dot, and it turns into a line. Then you interlink this line with that line, and ta-da, you've got the picture all matched up!

t_ch: 很简单吧？你听懂了吗？
t_en: Easy enough, eh? You got all that?

t_ch: 听懂了。
t_en: Yep.

t_ch: 不愧是你！真是天赋异禀的无名客！
t_en: That's my Nameless! Simply radiating with talent!

t_ch: 这要是能听懂就活见鬼了。
t_en: I'd be insane if I understood any of that.

t_ch: 哎呀！管你听没听懂！玩就完了！
t_en: Bah! Who cares if you got it! Just play the game!

t_ch: 只要玩一把，你就什么都想起来了！
t_en: You'll remember everything once you play!

t_ch: 唉…我也想好好来一次教学，但是教学太浪费字数了，我们的预算不太付得起服务器运营费。
t_en: *sigh* ...I'd like to provide a proper tutorial too, but it expends too many words. Our budget can't afford the server costs.

t_ch: 不需要，我老玩家了。
t_en: No need, I'm a veteran.

t_ch: 那太好了。教学太浪费字数了，我们的预算不太付得起服务器运营费。
t_en: Fantastic. Tutorials use up too many words, and we can't afford the server costs on our budget.

t_ch: 你们说话不能超过<unbreak>800</unbreak>字？
t_en: Your dialogue can't exceed <unbreak>800</unbreak> words?

t_ch: 是呀，虚拟陪玩会确保自己和你的对话不超过<unbreak>1000</unbreak>字。你肯定很好奇原因吧？
t_en: Yep. Virtual NPCs will ensure that the exchange between you and them won't exceed <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> words. I bet you're curious as to why?

t_ch: 答案很简单。因为我们预算有限，虚拟陪玩服务器只用得起免费体验版。
t_en: The answer's simple. We've got a tight budget. We've only managed to use the free trial version for the virtual NPC servers.

t_ch: 和你的对话超过<unbreak>1000</unbreak>字，那就要付费了。
t_en: It'll cost us if our conversation exceeds <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> words.

t_ch: 所以我们特意找人写了个对话逻辑——只要对话超过<unbreak>800</unbreak>字，虚拟陪玩就会尽快结束对话，确保不会不小心超过付费线。
t_en: So we specially got someone to write our conversation logic — once a conversation exceeds <unbreak>800</unbreak> words, the virtual NPC will end the conversation as soon as possible to avoid having to pay for excess fees.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，是不是很机智？
t_en: Heh-heh, pretty smart, no?

t_ch: 糟糕，又超<unbreak>800</unbreak>字了！你自己慢慢玩吧，我先撤了！
t_en: Damn, more than <unbreak>800</unbreak> words again! You have fun on your own, I'm off!

t_ch: 真是突兀的退场……
t_en: Such an abrupt exit...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}祖宗，您来玩摆货游戏了吗？您里面请，玩法入口在里面，祖宗。
t_en: Top dog {NICKNAME}, here to play the goods stacking game? Come in, the game entrance is right inside, top dog.

t_ch: 斯科特也把形象授权给你们了？
t_en: Skott also gave you his image rights?

t_ch: 的确如此，祖宗。
t_en: That is correct, top dog.

t_ch: 你这形象经过人家授权了吗？
t_en: Did he even give you his image rights?

t_ch: 当然经过了！
t_en: Of course he did!

t_ch: 斯科特这人其实某种意义上很好说话，只要钱给够，什么都有得谈，祖宗。
t_en: Skott is actually fairly easy to negotiate with — he'll do anything for the right price, top dog.

t_ch: 我还是喜欢你桀骜不驯的样子。
t_en: I still prefer that curt demeanor of yours.

t_ch: 你把「祖宗」当句号用了？
t_en: You end every sentence with "top dog"?

t_ch: 我明白了，祖宗。您不喜欢我现在这套说话方式。那我恢复一下，祖宗。
t_en: Understood, top dog. You dislike my current conversational format. Allow me to restore myself then, top dog.

t_ch: 你怎么又跑来摆货了？在金人巷给人打工还不够，现在专门跑到庙会来打工了是吧？
t_en: Why're you here to stack goods again? What, didn't tire of running errands at Aurum Alley, itching for more sweaty labor at the temple fair?

t_ch: 你要是剩余价值太多，不如让我榨取一下，别浪费在这种垃圾小游戏上。
t_en: If you've got value to spare, why not let me squeeze the rest out of you. Don't waste your time on this trash game.

t_ch: 味儿对了！
t_en: That's my Skott!

t_ch: 呃…也许别那么嚣张？
t_en: Err... Perhaps a little too intense?

t_ch: 不管怎么说，我都是这交感幻戏里的虚拟陪玩。就算态度再恶劣，也必须对你有求必应。这破差事干的多糟心啊！
t_en: Doesn't matter. I'm a virtual NPC in this Empathic Immersia. I'm still at your beck and call regardless of my attitude. This whole affair is so annoying!

t_ch: 那你给我讲个笑话吧。
t_en: Tell me a joke then.

t_ch: 切！真拿你没办法！我只讲一个笑话哦，多了我可不讲！
t_en: Tsk! Fine! I'm only gonna do one, and just one!

t_ch: 话说有一天，老师向小白、小红和小绿提了一个问题：「把你家里最巨大的东西带来学校。」
t_en: One day, the teacher asked Blanca, Scarlett, and Vernon a question: "Bring the biggest thing in your house to school."

t_ch: 第二天，小白搬来了家里的冰箱，那确实很大。小红开来了父亲的货车，比小白家的冰箱还要大。
t_en: The next day, Blanca brought her fridge to school, and boy it was a big one. Scarlett drove her dad's truck to school, and it was definitely bigger than Blanca's fridge.

t_ch: 这时大家看向小绿。他两手空空。于是老师就问：「小绿，你带了什么呀？」
t_en: All eyes were on Vernon at this point. His hands were empty. So the teacher asked: "Vernon, what have you brought?"

t_ch: 小绿微微一笑，说道……
t_en: Vernon smiled, and said...

t_ch: 那你给我讲个恐怖故事吧。
t_en: Tell me a horror story then.

t_ch: 切！真拿你没办法！我这个故事超级可怕哦，小心别吓死你！
t_en: Tsk! Fine! This story of mine'll scare the color off of your skin, so watch out!

t_ch: 在一个深夜里，小红和小绿一起回家。两人走进一条无人的窄巷，小绿突然惊叫一声，拉着小红冲了出去。
t_en: In the deep night, Scarlett and Vernon were going home together. When they walked into a deserted narrow alley, Vernon suddenly yelped in surprise, dragging Scarlett with him out of the alley.

t_ch: 小绿拉着小红跑了好久，才停下脚步。他浑身颤抖，一句话也说不出来。
t_en: Pulling on Scarlett's hand, Vernon ran for a long time before stopping. His whole body was shivering, and he was at a loss for words.

t_ch: 小红恐惧地问道：「刚才到底发生什么了？」
t_en: Terrified, Scarlett asked: "What happened back there?"

t_ch: 小绿指着两人刚刚跑过的那条窄巷，用颤抖的声音说……
t_en: Vernon pointed to that narrow alley they just ran past, and in a quivering tone, he said...

t_ch: 啊，咱们的对话超过<unbreak>800</unbreak>字了。本次对话到此结束。你快去摆货吧，再见。
t_en: Ah, we've exceeded <unbreak>800</unbreak> words! This chat will end now. Go stack your goods, goodbye.

t_ch: 你给我回来！小绿到底说了什么啊！给我说清楚啊！
t_en: Get back here! What exactly did Vernon say! No cliffhangers!

t_ch: 嗨，{NICKNAME}玩家。你想我了吗？想我就来玩紧张刺激的「枘凿六合•Remake」吧！
t_en: Hi, player {NICKNAME}. Did you miss me? Come play the thrilling Hexanexus: Remake if you did!

t_ch: 你不是在吃牢饭吗？
t_en: Aren't you eating prison goop?

t_ch: 囚犯也能授权形象？
t_en: Prisoners can also grant their image rights?

t_ch: 呵，这一整套交感幻戏的系统都是我开发的，他们不让我出来露个脸，那不成卸磨杀驴了吗？
t_en: Heh, I developed this entire Empathic Immersia system. It'd be absurd if they didn't even allow me to show my face, would it?

t_ch: 这套「虚拟陪玩」系统，由阿合马负责台本包装，由我负责功能实现，所以你现在是在直面这款交感幻戏的开发者！
t_en: This virtual NPC system was made possible with Arma Correctional Group's writing and my development. You stand before the great creator of this Empathic Immersia!

t_ch: 怎么样？你没有什么感想吗？
t_en: What do you think? Any thoughts?

t_ch: 能换点我没玩过的游戏吗？
t_en: You got something I haven't played before?

t_ch: 等有产能吧！到时候一定给你做有趣又好玩的新玩法！
t_en: When we've got more manpower! I'll definitely create something fun, interesting, and innovative for you!

t_ch: 那阿合马怎么没出来露脸？
t_en: Why hasn't Arma shown himself then?

t_ch: 那家伙长得有点过于…宝相庄严，容易吓到庙会的客人，实在不适合当引导员。
t_en: That fellow looks a little... imposingly dignified. We fear he might scare off temple fair guests, so he's really not suitable to be a guide.

t_ch: 能跳过玩法吗？我只想要奖励。
t_en: Can I skip the gameplay? I just want the reward.

t_ch: 别这样，求你了。你象征性玩一玩也好，帮我提升一下触达率，这样也算是有点立功减刑的依据。
t_en: C'mon, please. You can at least fiddle with the game for a while and help me boost my interaction rates. That will at least reduce my sentence a little.

t_ch: 你也是枘凿六合的老玩家了，我这引导员也确实没什么能「引导」的。
t_en: You're also a Hexanexus veteran. There's nothing much that I can guide as a... guide.

t_ch: 我先撤了，你慢慢玩吧。希望你喜欢这次的谜题设计。
t_en: I'm off, have fun. Hope you liked the design of this puzzle.

t_ch: 你还是再给我介绍下规则吧。
t_en: You'd probably better tell me about the rules again.

t_ch: 不行，再说下去要超过<unbreak>800</unbreak>字了，再见！
t_en: Nope, we're almost at the <unbreak>800</unbreak>-word limit. Goodbye!

t_ch: 你怎么废话变少了？
t_en: I noticed you've cut down on your blather.

t_ch: 因为我终于发现了——废话越少，好评率越高。
t_en: Because I've finally realized — the fewer words spoken, the higher my positive reviews.

t_ch: 这哥们还挺逗的。下次有时间再去牢里看看他吧。
t_en: This fella's pretty funny. I should go visit him the next time I'm going to the prison.

t_ch: 恭喜啊，{NICKNAME}！你成为了咱们庙会速通大赛的普通难度亚军！
t_en: Congrats, {NICKNAME}! You're our Normal Mode runner-up at our temple fair speedrun competition!

t_ch: 一共就两个选手…
t_en: There were only two competitors...

t_ch: 不要对自己那么苛刻嘛！你是货真价实的亚军～
t_en: Come now, give yourself a pat on your back! You're a bona fide runner-up!

t_ch: 好耶！我是亚军！
t_en: Aw yeah! I came in second!

t_ch: 没错！你就是货真价实的亚军！
t_en: That's right! You're a bona fide runner-up!

t_ch: 那冠军是谁？
t_en: Who's the champion?

t_ch: 当然是我。不然还能是谁？
t_en: Me, of course. Who else could it be?

t_ch: 我刚还在想呢，这无人问津的交感幻戏里怎么出现了另一个真人玩家…想来除了{NICKNAME}，也不会有别人这么闲了。
t_en: And here I was thinking, why is there another real player in this abandoned Empathic Immersia... I guess no one else would be this bored besides you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 原来二位早就认识！我就知道，游戏领域大神之间是会互相吸引的。
t_en: So you know each other! I called it — GOATs of the game world clop together.

t_ch: 真难为你玩这个烂游戏了。
t_en: It must be hard for you, playing this bad game.

t_ch: 烂游戏？不，这些小游戏有不少可圈可点的设计。
t_en: Bad game? Not really. These mini-games actually have quite a few design aspects worth noting.

t_ch: 你怎么会来「罗浮」？
t_en: What are you doing on the Luofu?

t_ch: 听说你要参加演武仪典，某两个家伙非让我过来看看你顺不顺利…麻烦死了，她们怎么不自己来？
t_en: I heard you were joining the Wardance, and two whose-names-I-won't-mention insisted that I checked in on you... So annoying. Why couldn't those girls make the trip themselves?

t_ch: 顺便一说，你刚才通关的是简单模式。这交感幻戏还有个困难模式，我也稍微多花了点心思才通关。
t_en: I'd also like to add that you only cleared Easy Mode. This Empathic Immersia also has a Hard Mode — I spent a little more time clearing those levels.

t_ch: 我觉得还挺好玩的，你要是有兴趣也可以试试。
t_en: I think it's pretty fun. You can give it a shot if you're interested.

t_ch: 没错！困难模式当前的速通冠军是银狼小姐！亚军会花落谁家呢？真令人期待啊！
t_en: That's right! The speedrun champion for hard mode is Miss Silver Wolf! Who will our runner-up be? How exciting!

t_ch: 我就不能超越她成为冠军？
t_en: Why can't I beat her and become champion?

t_ch: 哈哈，做梦。
t_en: Haha, in your dreams.

t_ch: 会花落谁家呢？好期待呀！
t_en: Who will it be? So exciting!

t_ch: 你们不要玩尬的了。我真的有点脚趾扣地。
t_en: You need to stop with the awkward lines. It's making me cringe.

t_ch: 总之看到你还算顺利，我的任务也就完成了。
t_en: Anyway, it's good to see you're still in one piece. That's mission accomplished for me.

t_ch: 我还想再逛几家幻戏馆，你在这里慢慢玩吧。等我有空了再来找你。
t_en: I'm going to stop by at a few more immersia theaters, so you take your time and have fun. I'll come look for you when I've got time.

t_ch: 大神您慢走！
t_en: Take care, champ!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，要不我现在就给您开通困难模式的权限？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, would you like me to activate Hard mode for you now?

t_ch: 我已经等不及了。
t_en: I can hardly wait.

t_ch: 恭喜你！成为了咱们庙会速通大赛的困难难度亚军！
t_en: Congrats! You're our Hard Mode runner-up at our temple fair speedrun competition!

t_ch: 都说了只有两个选手…
t_en: I already told you. There were only two contenders...

t_ch: 两个选手怎么了？你觉得一般玩家有资格做我的亚军吗？自信点。
t_en: So what if there are only two contenders? Do you think any average player is qualified to be my runner-up? Be more confident!

t_ch: 我早晚会战胜银狼！
t_en: I'll defeat Silver Wolf sooner or later!

t_ch: 游戏这条赛道你应该是没机会了。下次有机会比点别的吧。大胃王之类的？你也许能战胜我。
t_en: You probably don't have any chance to defeat me in games. Maybe we can compete in other things next time. What about stuff like competitive eating? Maybe you'd stand a chance.

t_ch: 好耶！我是亚军！
t_en: Aw yeah! I came in second!

t_ch: 喔，你通关速度比我想象中快多了。认真讲，还挺厉害的。
t_en: Woah, you're clearing levels faster than I thought. You're pretty good, if I'm being honest.

t_ch: 银狼小姐？你怎么回来了？
t_en: Miss Silver Wolf? Why are you back?

t_ch: 我在幻戏里注入的监测木马显示有人通关了困难模式，就回来祝贺一下。
t_en: The monitoring trojan that I injected into the immersia notified me that someone had cleared Hard Mode, so I came to offer my congratulations.

t_ch: 啊？注入…监测木马…？
t_en: Eh? You injected... a monitoring trojan...?

t_ch: 别担心，没有任何危害。而且我刚刚已经删除掉了——木马和证据都删干净了。
t_en: Don't worry, it's harmless. I've just deleted it — both the trojan and any evidence.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…其实无所谓啦，反正玩家也只有你们两个。估计以后也不会有第三个玩家了。
t_en: Hahaha... It's fine. You two are the only players anyway. I don't think there will be a third in the future.

t_ch: 不过说实话，仙舟这些解谜小游戏还是挺好玩的。这庙会明年还办吗？
t_en: Honestly, these little puzzles from Xianzhou are pretty fun. Are you gonna do this temple fair again next year?

t_ch: 我也不知道，但应该还要继续办吧。毕竟这也是延续一万年的传统了，可不能说不办就不办。
t_en: Beats me, but I bet we will keep it going. I mean, it's a tradition that's been carried on for <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> years. We can't just stop doing it all of a sudden.

t_ch: 那就行。下次再办记得叫我。
t_en: Good to hear. Remember to invite me next time.

t_ch: 就别叫我了。
t_en: Please don't invite me again.

t_ch: 无所谓，我自己玩也一样。
t_en: Doesn't matter. I can play by myself just the same.

t_ch: 也记得叫我。
t_en: Make sure to invite me too.

t_ch: 好啊。到时再让你看看，谁才是真正的益智小游戏之神！
t_en: Sure. I'll show you who's the real god of puzzle games.

t_ch: 唉…真感谢你们，不嫌弃我们这成本低廉、制作简陋的烂游戏。
t_en: Ah... I'm so thankful for you two. Thanks for not minding our low-budget trash game.

t_ch: 别这么说。你们的游戏虽然不是大制作，但玩起来也挺有意思的。
t_en: Don't say that. Even if your game isn't some Triple-A production, it's still pretty fun to play.

t_ch: 我走啦！还有正事要办。{NICKNAME}、召夏，后会有期。
t_en: I'm heading off! I still have some business to attend to. {NICKNAME}, Zhaoxia, see you later.

t_ch: 哇！这个魔方真的好好玩耶！
t_en: Wow! This magic cube is so fun!

t_ch: 枘凿六合天下第一！
t_en: Hexanexus is the best!

t_ch: 先这样转一下，再那样转一下，成了！
t_en: First turn it like this, then like that, done!

t_ch: 难度不够高，开发组什么时候出三阶魔方？
t_en: It isn't difficult enough. When will the devs release the Phase Three cube?

t_ch: 真的耶！枘凿六合真是世界上最好玩的游戏！
t_en: You're right! Hexanexus truly is the most enjoyable game in the world!

t_ch: 打通这关就下线。
t_en: Just one more level...

t_ch: 原来你也玩枘凿六合啊。
t_en: I didn't know you played Hexanexus too.

t_ch: 什么？我一顿瞎操作居然解开了？
t_en: What? I randomly messed around and it solved itself?

t_ch: 今年的年度游戏就是它了！
t_en: The game of the year is gonna be this!

t_ch: 天呐，世界上怎么有这么好玩的游戏！
t_en: OMG, how can there be such a fun game in this world?

t_ch: 我愿称这个游戏为业界奇迹。
t_en: I will call this game a game industry miracle.

t_ch: 以我三百年老玩家的经验，这游戏要火啊。
t_en: With my three hundred years of gaming experience, I bet this game is gonna go viral!

t_ch: 神作啊，绝对的神作！
t_en: Legendary, this is absolutely legendary!

t_ch: 发明这个游戏的人真是天才！
t_en: The inventor of this game must be a genius!

t_ch: 这个游戏治好了我的电子游戏倦怠症。
t_en: This game cured my video game fatigue.

t_ch: 我愿给这个游戏打出满分。
t_en: I can only give this game a perfect score.

t_ch: 赞美大衍万象！
t_en: Praise Mutare Magnus!

t_ch: 没有比玩大衍万象更幸福的事。
t_en: There's nothing better than playing Mutare Magnus.

t_ch: 还差最后一步就解开啦。
t_en: One last step to unlocking it.

t_ch: 这把游戏我优势很大。
t_en: I have a huge advantage in this round.

t_ch: 这关难不倒我。
t_en: This level is nothing to me.

t_ch: 活着就是为了玩大衍万象。
t_en: The meaning of life is to play Mutare Magnus.

t_ch: 这精美的画质，创新的玩法，精妙的关卡！
t_en: Such exquisite graphics, innovative gameplay, and clever level design!

t_ch: 来局大衍万象吧！
t_en: Come play a round of Mutare Magnus!

t_ch: 怎么总差几个货物啊？
t_en: Why are there always a few items missing?

t_ch: 好玩，爱玩。
t_en: Fun. I love it.

t_ch: 是时候展现我真正的技术了。
t_en: Time for a true display of skill.

t_ch: 好精妙的设计，居然把我都难住了。
t_en: What an amazing design. Can't believe it's tripping me up.

t_ch: 一天不码放货物我就浑身难受。
t_en: I get all irritated if I don't arrange items every day.

t_ch: 差点摆错，还好我技高一筹。
t_en: Almost set it wrong. Luckily my skills are superior.

t_ch: 这个游戏到底是谁在赢啊！
t_en: Who is winning in this game!?

t_ch: 好难，还是看一眼攻略吧。
t_en: So difficult. Let me take a look at the guide.

t_ch: 嘘。这边——
t_en: Shh. Over here...

t_ch: 抱歉。我不想引起周围的注意…最近在学院里过得还愉快吗？
t_en: Sorry, I didn't want to attract any attention... Have you been having fun at school lately?

t_ch: 将蕉授从梦境中驱逐出去后，折纸大学很快恢复了原本的秩序。你过来的路上应该看到了，学生们都在热火朝天地准备「彩梦校庆」的音乐派对呢。
t_en: After the Profnana was exiled from the dreamscape, Paperfold University has since quickly restored its former order. You should've seen it along the way — the students are in a frenzy prepping for the Dreamlight Anniversary's musical party.

t_ch: …音乐派对？
t_en: ...music party?

t_ch: 这是校庆中最盛大的一项活动。来自不同学院的学生们会在派对上带来一场精彩的演出，用音乐来展示折纸大学的活力。
t_en: This is a most momentous event in our school celebrations. Students from different schools will present an exciting performance during the party, to exhibit Paperfold University's vigor through music.

t_ch: 虽说是学生活动，但学院会提供充分的支持，因此也备受各大家系关注。
t_en: It may be a student event, but the academy provides ample support, which is why it's so closely followed by the various departments.

t_ch: 谢谢，开拓者很喜欢。
t_en: Thank you. The Trailblazer loves it.

t_ch: 那就好，蕉授制造的混乱已经结束了，真正的校长好像也回到了学校——现在总算能安心享受轻松的校园生活了。
t_en: That's good to hear. The chaos that the Profnana sowed is over, and the true dean has also returned — everyone can finally bask in their carefree campus lives.

t_ch: 有空的话，我陪你在学校里到处看看吧。派对会用到的设施都还在搭建中，但气氛已经相当热闹了，和你刚到时候的感受肯定完全不一样。
t_en: If you're free, we can do a tour of the school. The facilities used in the party are still being built, but the atmosphere's really lively. It's way different from what you experienced earlier.

t_ch: 音乐就是我的全部！
t_en: Music is our everything!

t_ch: 社团的宗旨是——
t_en: The society's creed is...

t_ch: 让梦境变得更热闹！
t_en: To make the dreamscape more bustling!

t_ch: 就要上台了，好紧张…
t_en: Almost our turn to go on. I'm so nervous...

t_ch: 贝斯果然没存在感。
t_en: The bassist is always the most invisible one.

t_ch: 我会拼命演唱的！
t_en: I will play my heart out!

t_ch: 表演还没开始吗？
t_en: Has the performance not started yet?

t_ch: 为什么要演奏这首…
t_en: Why're we playing this song...

t_ch: 给你看，这是我刚才拍的照片！
t_en: Here, a picture I just took for you!

t_ch: 这幅涂鸦的形状好特别，究竟是谁画的，有什么含义呢？
t_en: This graffiti art is so unique. Who did it? What does it mean?

t_ch: 那…涂鸦的作者，应该是个很有礼貌的人？
t_en: Then... I guess that graffiti artist is probably very polite?

t_ch: 我们去请这个人，来给社团活动画海报吧！
t_en: Let's go invite that person to make a poster for the club event!

t_ch: 我是不是在哪见过…
t_en: Where have I seen this...

t_ch: 这不是乱破画的吗！
t_en: Isn't this Rappa's style?

t_ch: 咦，这位猴友，你认识涂鸦的作者？带我们去找他好不好！
t_en: Oh, you know this graffiti artist, fine monkey friend? How about taking us to him!

t_ch: 我们是「观忍社」的成员，负责运营这次音乐派对的主播账号，正在寻找能给社团虚拟形象画海报的人…要是你知道什么的话，请告诉我们！
t_en: We're members of the Ninja Appreciation Society, charged with running the music party's livestream account. We're looking for someone who can draw posters for the society's virtual avatar... Please tell us if you've got any leads!

t_ch: …怎么还是猴友啊？
t_en: ...Why are we still dealing with monkey friends?

t_ch: 哎呀，这就是个最近流行的梗而已啦，别太较真。
t_en: Sheesh, it's just a recently trending meme. Stop being such a stickler.

t_ch: 不过这个梗确实过气了…或许我们该叫你「忍友」！
t_en: But this meme is probably outdated... Maybe we should call you "friendja" instead!

t_ch: …什么是「观忍社」？
t_en: ...What's the Ninja Appreciation Society?

t_ch: 哼哼哼，跟不上潮流了吧？现在舞台上的忍者都需要一副名为「虚偃」的伪装，修行「虚偃•拟观幻术」的社团就是「观忍社」！
t_en: Heh-heh-heh, not keeping up with the times, eh? All ninja on stage all need a Void Disguise these days, and the society that trains in the Void Pseudo-Watch-jutsu is the Ninja Appreciation Society!

t_ch: 而社团成员——我们，就是品鉴虚偃的「观虚忍者」，简称「观忍」！
t_en: And the society members, we, are the Void Ninja Watchers who evaluate the Void — more succinctly known as "Watcher Ninjas"!

t_ch: 这种忍术是初代兼永世社长在课堂上教给我们的，相当好用，好多人争抢着想要入社，我们都没答应呢。
t_en: This ninjutsu was taught to us by our first generation and eternal society chief. Many are clamoring to gain admission into the society, but none have been approved by us.

t_ch: 可惜，社长在教学结束后就消失了，到现在都不知道她的名字。
t_en: Alas, our chief disappeared after the lesson ended, and to this day, no one knows her name.

t_ch: 你们社长，是不是粉色头发…
t_en: Does your chief have pink hair...

t_ch: 哇，这位猴友居然知道社长！看来我们的忍术流派正在发扬光大啊。
t_en: Woah, our monkey friend knows the chief! Our ninjutsu school is getting quite popular, it seems.

t_ch: 你们社团，都有些什么活动？
t_en: What activities does your society have?

t_ch: 唔…画立绘、运营账号、和弹幕互动、发放抽选券、开付费直播。
t_en: Hmm... drawing, running our socials, bullet barrage interactions, giving out lottery tickets, and hosting paid streams.

t_ch: 想学？我教你啊，得先通过我们的观忍试炼才行！
t_en: Care to learn? I'll teach you, but you'll have to pass our Watcher Ninja trial first!

t_ch: …网络主播啊，这个我熟！
t_en: ...Internet livestreaming, I'm familiar with that!

t_ch: 不瞒你们说，我也曾是拥有百万粉丝的大主播。
t_en: Truth be told, I was once a major streamer with millions of fans.

t_ch: 你掏出手机，向两名学生自豪展示了「小桂子Guinevere」——这个承载着你诸多珍贵回忆的网络账号。
t_en: You whip out your phone and proudly show off LilGuiGuinevere to the two students — an account that bears many of your precious memories.

t_ch: 啊？你就是小桂子本人？可我听说小桂子是个活力四射的小姑娘啊？
t_en: Eh? You're Lil Gui herself? I thought Lil Gui was a sprightly young lady, though?

t_ch: 下播就换了一个人，难道说——我悟了，你才是真正的「观忍大师」！
t_en: But you're a totally different person, unless... I get it, you're the real master Watcher Ninja!

t_ch: 这段话的槽点实在有点多…
t_en: There are quite a few loopholes with this line...

t_ch: 所以，你能带我们找到这幅涂鸦的作者吗？
t_en: So, can you take us to this graffiti artist?

t_ch: 不知道这个人会不会对音乐派对感兴趣……
t_en: I don't know if this person would be interested in the music party...

t_ch: 劝你们还是别找她。
t_en: I don't think you should look for her.

t_ch: 啧，该不会是怕你们社团的成员被「观忍社」抢走吧？小气！
t_en: Tsk, afraid that your society members would be snatched up by the Ninja Appreciation Society? So petty!

t_ch: …我尝试着问问吧。
t_en: ...I'll try asking.

t_ch: 行呀，要是有什么消息，请随时和我们「观忍社」联系！
t_en: Nice, if you have any news, please contact us, the Ninja Appreciation Society!

t_ch: 我想在这个人画的涂鸦前演奏！
t_en: I want to play a piece in front of this artist's graffiti!

t_ch: 现在的学生都很有个性呢。和他们接触的时候，总是会怀疑是不是自己的想法过时了。
t_en: Students now sure have plenty of personality. When I interact with them, I always wonder if I'm outdated.

t_ch: 还挺讨人厌的…
t_en: It's pretty annoying...

t_ch: 不过也多亏了这份热情，音乐派对才能顺利举办。
t_en: But it's all thanks to this passion that the music party is going smoothly.

t_ch: 我超喜欢这里！
t_en: I love this place!

t_ch: 是吧。多亏了这份热情，音乐派对才能顺利举办。
t_en: Right? It's thanks to this passion that the music party is up and running.

t_ch: 其实，原本折纸大学有考虑过停办这次活动。蕉授引发的混乱才刚平息，又适逢匹诺康尼上市的关键时期，谁都不愿惹出更多事。
t_en: Actually, Paperfold University considered stopping this event. The ruckus that Profnana caused has just been quelled, and it's also the pivotal moment immediately after Penacony became listed. No one really wants any more trouble to arise.

t_ch: 是学生们集体向家族递交申请，保证会办好音乐派对。经过多方协商和反复衡量，学院才勉强同意。因此，这场活动也受到了特别的关注。
t_en: It was the students who submitted their request to The Family, promising that the music party would be a success. It was after many rounds of negotiation and deliberation that the Academy finally acquiesced. This is also why this event is of particular attention.

t_ch: 你是怎么看的呢？
t_en: What do you make of this?

t_ch: 和我说这些是想…
t_en: Why are you telling me all this...

t_ch: 可能因为派对主题是音乐吧，我非常希望这场活动能正常举办。通过旋律来表达自我的愿望不应该被否定，更不应受到上层事务的影响。
t_en: Probably because the party's theme is tied to music, I really hope that this event will be organized uneventfully. The wish to express oneself through melody should not be denied, nor should it be deprived of by matters of the higher echelon.

t_ch: 音乐述说着最纯粹的梦，这才是匹诺康尼作为*梦想之地*的真实含义。
t_en: Music conveys the purest of dreams, and this is the true meaning of Penacony as the Land of the Dreams.

t_ch: 所以——你想加入这场音乐派对吗？
t_en: So... do you wish to participate in the music party?

t_ch: …我来表演？诶，真的假的？
t_en: ...Me? Put on a show? Really?

t_ch: 是时候展现我的音乐天赋了！
t_en: It's time to flaunt my musical chops!

t_ch: 虽然很期待你的舞台表现，可惜，这次似乎没有机会呢。
t_en: While I look forward to your stage performance, it appears that I may not have that chance this time.

t_ch: 目前派对的大部分事宜都已经准备完毕，唯独最重要的三场演出还没有进展。
t_en: Most of the party's preparatory matters have been completed. The only and most important issue is the three performances, which have seen no progress.

t_ch: 届时，校园的中央舞台会有一支由学生组成的乐队进行演奏，但始终没找到合适的筹办人选。不知道你有没有兴趣，<color=#dbc291ff>担任这支乐队的经理呢</color>？
t_en: When the time comes, there will be a student band playing on the school's central stage, but we haven't found any suitable candidates. I wonder if you're interested in <color=#dbc291ff>becoming this band's manager?</color>

t_ch: 乐队经理要做些什么？
t_en: What does a band manager do?

t_ch: 主要就是组织排练，为正式演出做准备吧。别担心，乐队成员已经确定好了，都是各个学院选出的优秀学生，不会让你为难的。
t_en: Mainly to organize practice and prepare for the actual performance. Don't worry. The band members have all been confirmed — they're all outstanding students that the various institutes have selected, and they won't trouble you at all.

t_ch: 需要进行三场演出吗？
t_en: There must be three performances?

t_ch: 这次音乐派对会持续很长时间，按现在的规划，在中央舞台进行三场演出，正好能维持校园的欢庆气氛，也不至于给乐队过分的压力。
t_en: The music party this round will last for quite some time. According to current plans, the central stage will have three performances — aptly so to maintain the mirth of the school celebration, while also not overly stressing the band out.

t_ch: 其中，最后的那场演出会是音乐派对的闭幕表演，成为所有人的焦点。
t_en: Among these, the last show will be the music party's closing performance, which will be the highlight for everyone.

t_ch: 好突然，我有点紧张…
t_en: So sudden, I'm a little nervous...

t_ch: 没事的。作为经理不需要登台，保证乐队演出能顺利进行就好。
t_en: Don't be. The manager doesn't have to be on stage. They just need to ensure the band's performance goes without a hitch.

t_ch: 自己带领的乐队受到万众瞩目，在舞台上闪闪发光，这么想是不是轻松多啦？
t_en: All eyes on a band that you lead, dazzling with splendor on stage. Who wouldn't be at ease at the mere thought of that?

t_ch: 为什么要找我呢？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 都认识这么长时间了，我当然愿意相信你的品行和能力。
t_en: We've known each other a long time. Of course I'm going to trust in your conduct and capability.

t_ch: 并且…背后还有些更复杂的缘由。正好快到学院办公室了，请随我过来再说吧。
t_en: And... there are some more complicated threads behind this matter. We're almost at the academy office, so please come with me so we can talk about this more.

t_ch: …这件事不能由投票决定！艺术是天才的特权，三占从二只会毁了宝贵的才华！
t_en: ...This matter cannot be decided by a vote! Art is a genius' special right — a two-thirds majority will only ruin precious talent!

t_ch: 这种幼稚的言论还是留到你的论文里吧。作为学院负责人，你应该清楚这场音乐派对的价值，不是什么艺术殿堂，而是匹诺康尼在市场上竞争的筹码。
t_en: Please save this childish rhetoric for your thesis. You should be clear on the valuable import of this music party — it isn't some hallowed artistic hall, but a bargaining chip in the Penaconian market.

t_ch: 繁荣、稳定、充满生机的梦境才能吸引全银河的投资者，名义上由学生主办的活动无疑是最好的展示窗口。我们不能错过这个机会。
t_en: Only a prosperous, stable and vibrant Dreamscape can attract investors from all over the cosmos. An event ostensibly organized by students is indubitably the greatest exhibition showcase. We cannot miss this opportunity.

t_ch: 因此，我强烈建议由财富学院的学生担任乐队核心，进而把控派对的整体效果。
t_en: Which is why, I strongly recommend the Fortune Academy's student undertake the task of being the band leader, to control the overall impact of the party.

t_ch: 请注意：这里是校园，培育精英的场所理应由最杰出的头脑领导。
t_en: Please be reminded: This is an academy. A venue that nurtures the elite should undoubtedly be led by the brightest minds.

t_ch: 绩点是衡量学员能力最为有效的方式。绩点最高的学员，将成为本次乐队演出的核心，如此的选拔体系不会招致任何非议。
t_en: Grades are the most effective method in gauging a student's ability. The student with the best grade will become this band leader. Such a selection process will not invite any unwanted criticism.

t_ch: 哼。破铁罐子也敢在这和我谈音乐，可笑至极！
t_en: Hmph. You dare debate me on music theory, you heap of scrap! This is a bad joke!

t_ch: …这是什么情况？
t_en: ...What's going on?

t_ch: 打起来！打起来！
t_en: Fight! Fight!

t_ch: 哟，这不是最受学生欢迎的大明星——知更鸟小姐嘛？既然你在集会上力排众议，坚持要举办音乐派对，那就给大家出个主意呗？
t_en: Whoa there, if it isn't the superstar that the kids like? Miss Robin, was it? During the assembly, you were vehemently outspoken on your insistence to organize the music party. Do you have any good ideas?

t_ch: 演出乐队的核心位置，究竟该由哪个学院的学生担任？
t_en: The performing band is a key position. Which institute should undertake this crucial role?

t_ch: 相信各位代表在此反复磋商，都是为了折纸大学的整体利益考虑，希望将此次音乐派对打造得尽善尽美。既然如此，我有个全新的提议——
t_en: I believe the reasons put forth by every rep are all for the sake of Paperfold University's overall benefit. I hope this music party will run like a perfectly well-oiled machine. In that case, I have a new proposal...

t_ch: 大家各退一步，将决定权交给我身边的这位无名客。这样如何？
t_en: Everyone take a step back, and leave the final decision to this Nameless beside me. How about it?

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 要不…丢个骰子试试？
t_en: How about... deciding with a die roll?

t_ch: 音乐好处都有啥，说对就给他！
t_en: What are the merits of music? I'll give it to whoever gets it right!

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}？我从来没在学校里见过这号人，凭什么把决定权交给{F#她}{M#他}？
t_en: {F#Her}{M#Him}? I've never seen this person in the school before. Why should we bequeath such an authority to {F#her}{M#him}?

t_ch: 等等，这人好像是匹诺康尼的股东。
t_en: Hold on. I think this person is a stakeholder of Penacony.

t_ch: 股、股东？咳咳，这个事呢…不是我怀疑这位股东…但音乐派对作为咱们学院内部的传统活动，牵扯的层面太多，这里边的水太深了……
t_en: S—Stakeholder? Ahem, about this matter... It's not that I'm doubting this stakeholder... but the music party is a longstanding interschool tradition with many complex layers of nuances woven between. There is simply too much context to understand...

t_ch: 同学，给个面子。
t_en: Come on, cut me some slack.

t_ch: 你担心我把握不住？
t_en: Are you worried that I can't handle it?

t_ch: 让我整顿一下就不深了。
t_en: Allow me to make it less complicated.

t_ch: 不是，您多理解一下，这是学生内部的事务，理应由……
t_en: No, please do understand. This is an internal student matter, and it should be handled by...

t_ch: ——苦心孤诣追逐音律潮流，蝇营狗苟绝非忍界所求！
t_en: We painstakingly pursue music trends, and shamelessly seeking personal gain is never the ninja's end!

t_ch: 缭乱忍侠•AKA乱破，参见诸位！
t_en: The Dazzling Ninja Hero AKA Rappa, humbly presents herself before you all!

t_ch: …这个搞行为艺术的，是你们谐乐学院的么？还真挺谐的。
t_en: ...Is this artist from your Charmony Academy? She's quite harmonious indeed.

t_ch: 非也。在下始终行于缭乱•忍法，从未师承过其他流派。
t_en: Nah. I have always practiced the Dazzling Ninjutsu and have never taken an apprenticeship at any other school.

t_ch: 方才听闻诸位正为音律祭典的忍首之位争论，若苦思不得法，不妨听在下一言。
t_en: I overheard that you all are deliberating the appointment of the Chief Ninja for the music festival. Should you find yourselves at an impasse, may I be permitted to offer a humble suggestion?

t_ch: 在下在折纸村中潜伏已有些许时日，得知此番「音律•祭典」乃是诸位忍徒翘首以盼的盛会，而对将要登台的「音律•忍徒」而言更是极为重要的忍道试炼……
t_en: I have been lurking within the Paperfold Town for some time, and learned that this music festival is a highly anticipated event among you. For the Sound Ninja poised to perform, this should be of utmost importance for one that seeks to further the way of the ninja.

t_ch: 所以，祭典的「忍首」应是能够肩负忍徒期望之人，不应由诸位用外物定夺！
t_en: Therefore, the Chief Ninja of the Festival should be one capable of shouldering the hopes of ninja way and not be decided by external means!

t_ch: …她说啥呢？
t_en: ...What is she saying?

t_ch: 检测到大量加密词汇，语言解析中…解析失败。
t_en: Massive amounts of encrypted vocabulary detected, language parsing in progress... Parsing failed.

t_ch: 我大概理解了，乱破小姐的意思是这场音乐派对是献给学生们的礼物，也是乐队成员们实现梦想的机会……
t_en: I believe I understand. What Ms. Rappa means is that this music party is a gift for the students, and an opportunity for band members to fulfill their dreams...

t_ch: 所以对于乐队而言，各位口中的才能、绩点、效益都不重要——重要的是这个人要能领导乐队，为学生们带来一场精彩的演出才行。
t_en: For a band, what truly matters is not talent, GPA, or efficiency, but the ability to lead the band and deliver a captivating performance for the audience.

t_ch: 我同意，这个校外人士都比你们两个代表要想得清楚！所以为了带来足够精彩的演出，乐队核心理应由我们最懂音乐的学院来……
t_en: I agree, this outsider thinks more clearly than you two representatives! To bring out an exciting performance, the band leader ought to be entrusted to our academy, which possesses the greatest understanding of music...

t_ch: 故此，就由在下担起全部重责吧！
t_en: Therefore, let me undertake all this responsibility!
WHAT!?

t_ch: 那么…本次音乐派对的演出乐队，乐队经理由来自星穹列车的无名客——{NICKNAME}担任，核心则是这位乱破小姐。
t_en: Then... For this music party's performing band, the band manager shall be the Astral Express' Nameless — {NICKNAME}. The band leader shall be Ms. Rappa.

t_ch: 各位学院代表对此还有意见吗？
t_en: Do the academy's representatives have any objections to this?

t_ch: 乱破…莫、莫非是《银河忍法帖》的主角？！
t_en: Rappa... C—Could it be that she is the protagonist of the Cosmic Ninjutsu Inscriptions?

t_ch: 赞成：这部电影是芮克先生的人气新作，其主演登台能带来更多关注。
t_en: Approval: This film is Mr. Reca's latest popular work, and the appearance of the lead actress on stage will surely attract more attention.

t_ch: 嘁，那就这样吧。继续争论也是浪费时间，有大腕儿愿意上台，观众肯定更愿意来看，我们自然不反对。
t_en: Tsk, so be it. It's just a waste of time to continue debating. If a big shot is willing to step on stage, the spectators will be more willing to watch. We naturally have no objections.

t_ch: 不过要提醒你，音乐派对可是学校的大事，必须办得让所有人满意才行。
t_en: But I have to remind you, the music party is a huge event for the school. It must be done in a way that satisfies everyone.

t_ch: 阁下无需多虑。在下的忍•修行中，恰好有一项与乐曲有关的奥术——名曰音律•忍法。
t_en: My lord's mind be at ease. I fortuitously chanced upon a secret art related to melody during my ninja training — the Sound Release ninjutsu.

t_ch: 至于维护流派声名，言传身教，更是在下应尽之责，诸位尽可安心。
t_en: As for upholding the repute of my school, it is my utmost duty to champion honor with word and action. Please rest easy.

t_ch: 全仰仗您。
t_en: I'm counting on you.

t_ch: 现在我也是制作人了！
t_en: Now I'm a producer too!

t_ch: 哟！{NICKNAME}•忍者，别来无恙…不对，此刻，应当称呼阁下为{RUBY_B#乐队经理}「音律•主公」{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Yo! Ninja {NICKNAME}, how do you do... Wait, I should presently address you as {RUBY_B#Band Manager}Sound Master{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 呵呵，那音乐派对的三场乐队演出，就拜托你们了。正好来自各个学院的乐队成员都在排练室做准备，现在就去和他们见见吧。
t_en: Heh-heh, I'm counting on you guys for the music party's band performances. The various academies' band members are getting ready in the rehearsal room. You should go see them.

t_ch: 歌舞•忍者，阁下就放心吧——缭乱•忍侠AKA乱破，定不辱使命。
t_en: Worry not, Songstress Ninja, my lord — the Dazzling Ninja Hero AKA Rappa never sullies her duty.

t_ch: 缭乱忍侠向在场的所有人行礼，乐队演出的相关事宜就这么顺理成章地决定了。
t_en: The Dazzling Ninja Hero bows to everyone, and the matter related to the band's performance is decided without much fuss.

t_ch: 在排练室，你与乱破观看了这几位乐队成员的练习场景。
t_en: In the rehearsal room, you and Rappa look over the band members practicing.

t_ch: 音律•忍徒们哟，在下方才观摩了诸位的演奏——
t_en: Sound Ninja, I have observed your performances...

t_ch: 恕在下直言——想要带来让忍徒们认可的祭典，诸位的音律•忍法修为还远远不够。
t_en: If I may be so bold — you may wish to deliver a festival that fellow ninjas will truly recognize, but your Sound Release ninjutsu is sorely lacking.

t_ch: 喂，你在胡说什么。就算是知更鸟小姐指定的队长，也不能这么打击人吧？
t_en: Hey, how dare you spew such tripe? Even if you were the leader appointed by Miss Robin, there's no need to be this harsh, is there?

t_ch: 莫要激动，《银河忍法帖》有云——「没有忍者在襁褓中就手持苦无」。无论诸位的天赋多么优越，修行也必不可少。
t_en: Stay your anger. The Cosmic Ninjutsu Inscriptions states: "No ninja bears a kunai in swaddled clothes." No matter how gifted you all may be, your training must match your talent.

t_ch: 既然在下接过了忍首职责，就必然会将诸位训练成无愧于「缭乱流」之名的杰出忍者！
t_en: Since I have undertaken the duty of Chief Ninja, I must train everyone into exemplar ninja worthy of the name of the Dazzling School!

t_ch: 正式修行前，劳烦诸位先介绍各自的身份与忍•特技，在下也好制定特训章程。
t_en: Before training proper, I would have everyone introduce themselves and their special ninja arts, which allows me to more easily tailor a special training schedule.

t_ch: 还未下定决心么？那便由主公做个示范吧。
t_en: Does indecision still cloud your minds? Let the Sound Master lead by example, then.

t_ch: （用正常的方式介绍自己）
t_en: (Introduce yourself in the most normal way)

t_ch: 呃…你们好，我是乐队的经理，很高兴和各位见面。大家共同努力，完成后续在音乐派对上的演出。本次的目标是——
t_en: Err... Hey everyone, I'm the band manager. Great to meet you all. Let's all work hard together and complete the music party performances. Our goal this time is to...

t_ch: 全梦境金奖！
t_en: Obtain the DREAMSCAPE GOLD MEDAL!

t_ch: 什么东西…我没听说过这个奖项。
t_en: What the heck... I've never heard of such an award.

t_ch: 名震寰宇！
t_en: Be world-famous!

t_ch: 是不是…难度有点太高了？
t_en: Isn't that... a little over our heads?

t_ch: 重在参与！
t_en: Revel in participation!

t_ch: 重在参与！开心就好！音乐万岁！喔喔——
t_en: Participation is key! What matters is that you have fun! All glory to music! Ohhhhh!!!

t_ch: 以上就是我的自我介绍，谢谢各位。
t_en: That's all for my self-intro. Thank you.

t_ch: 噢…噢。
t_en: Oh... Oh.

t_ch: （用忍•真言介绍自己）
t_en: (Introduce yourself using a ninja mantra)

t_ch: 不管你有没有听过，反正不是我瞎说。乐队经理由我当，大家谁都不用慌。三场演出才算完，各位都还有得忙。难！
t_en: Doesn't matter if you've heard this before, I'm not kidding around anyway. I'm the band manager, so no one needs to panic. We've got three shows to do, so everyone's got to get busy. It's gonna be hard!

t_ch: 我是——
t_en: I am...

t_ch: 银河球棒侠，全年无休不放假。
t_en: The Galactic Baseballer, and I've never taken a day off.

t_ch: 没看出来，你也是个怪人。
t_en: How surprising — you're a weirdo too.

t_ch: 列车无名客，星间旅行好快乐。
t_en: Astral Express Nameless, it's really fun to travel the stars.

t_ch: 旅行？能…带我一个吗？
t_en: Travel? Can... Can I join?

t_ch: 灰毛到此游，深藏功名不久留。
t_en: Gray-haired one here, taking names, kickin' butt, leaving before stuff gets drear.

t_ch: 留…留…音乐最解我心愁！
t_en: Ma... Making... music always relieves my burdens!

t_ch: 喔！阁下很有潜力啊！
t_en: Oof! The Sound Master has a knack for this!

t_ch: 自我介绍就到这，压力来到你们了！
t_en: That's it for my self-introduction. It's your turn now!

t_ch: 不就是聚会破冰吗，让我这个财富学院的「派对之王」先来吧！
t_en: Isn't it just breaking the ice at a party? Allow me, the Fortune Academy's Party King, to have a go first!

t_ch: 我~的~花~美丽的小姐啊，可否将目光稍微停驻，让你有充分的时间来记住我~你记住了我，又忘记了我。就像你遇见了我，终有天会离开我~
t_en: Oh~ my~ flower~ Beautiful lady, may your glimpse linger, leaving ample time to remember me~ You remember me, yet forget me. Just like you met me, and one day you shall leave me~

t_ch: 但请你牢记我的名字~契波•欧拉欧•拉欧~财富学院最优秀的「键盘手」~
t_en: But please remember my name, Czipo Lionel Lero! Fortune Academy's most outstanding keyboardist...

t_ch: 怎么还唱上了？
t_en: Why'd he start singing?

t_ch: 好可怕的音乐品味。
t_en: What frightening taste in music.

t_ch: 还好他不是主唱…
t_en: Good thing he's not lead vocals...

t_ch: 契波•欧拉欧…名号甚是绕口，忍号应当简洁有力——听好，阁下此后便是「键盘•忍徒」！
t_en: Czipo Lionel... Your name is certainly a mouthful. Ninja names should be crisp and powerful. Listen up, your name will henceforth be Ninja Initiate Keyboard!

t_ch: 哦！太帅了！
t_en: Ooh! Sounds amazing!

t_ch: 艾尔菲。吉他演奏。谐乐学院的学生。
t_en: Arfey. Guitarist. Student. Charmony Academy.

t_ch: 叫我的名字，我不需要什么忍号。
t_en: Call me by my name. I don't need any ninja nicknames.

t_ch: 「吉他•忍徒」，我等将要共同登台，理应有彼此相称的名号，也能令诸位忍徒牢记阁下的身姿。
t_en: Ninja Initiate Guitar, we will take to the stage together, and thus should possess names that befit our mutual standing, and also allow other ninja to carve the memory of your poise in their minds.

t_ch: …我是来正经演奏的。想搞无聊的炒作或者扮演游戏别来找我。
t_en: ...I'm here to perform a serious show. You can excuse me if you're into pointless hype and role playing.

t_ch: 我…我是筑梦学院的学生，负责弹贝斯…名字叫做…达凡绮。
t_en: I... I'm a Dreamweaver Academy student who plays the bass... My name is... Dafanchy.

t_ch: 班上的同学都会喊我…算了，没什么！嗯。就这些。
t_en: My classmates will call me... Never mind. It's nothing! Mm, that's all.

t_ch: 「贝斯•忍徒」，银河忍法虽千变万化，却万变不离其宗。有一条真言须时刻牢记，那便是——
t_en: Ninja Initiate Bass, Cosmic Ninjutsu may be mercurial, but never depart from its source. There is a mantra that you must always remember. That is...

t_ch: 念出名号时，嗓音务必响亮！气势要足以震慑对手心神！
t_en: When you say your name, your voice must be resounding! Your aura must send tremors through your opponent's spirit!

t_ch: 我…我！…我喊不出来……
t_en: I... I! ...I can't do it...

t_ch: 无需心急，经历过在下的修行，阁下定会有所长进。
t_en: Stay composed. Once you undergo my training, you will surely see improvement.

t_ch: 最后，我等缭乱•音忍的流派还需要一个大名，这就由我等的「主公」来决定吧！但要切记——<color=#dbc291ff>「缭乱」</color>的忍魂之名不可弃置！
t_en: Lastly, our Dazzling Sound Ninja school must have an official name. I will let our Sound Master decide! But remember — the ninja spiritual name of the <color=#dbc291ff>Dazzling</color> cannot be discarded!

t_ch: 就叫「缭乱乐团」吧！
t_en: Let's just call ourselves the Dazzling Band!

t_ch: 乐团…如此简洁的名号，质而不俚，阁下真是一位务实的主公。
t_en: Band... Such a succinct name, elegant yet unostentatious. The Sound Master is a most pragmatic leader indeed.

t_ch: 那么，这就是我等流派的大名了吗？
t_en: Then, this will be our school's official name?

t_ch: 就叫这个吧！
t_en: Let this be it!

t_ch: 让我再想想…
t_en: Give me another minute...

t_ch: 无妨，命名流派并非小事，阁下应当深思而后行。
t_en: Let us not be hasty. The naming of a school is a monumental task. My lord ought to be more prudent.

t_ch: 就叫「缭乱众」吧！
t_en: Let's call it the Dazzling Collective!

t_ch: 缭乱众…此番名号颇有忍者的风采，不愧是身负球棒•忍者之名的上忍。
t_en: Dazzling Collective... Such a name radiates with ninja countenance — you are a true advanced ninja upholding the Baseball Bat Ninja's name.

t_ch: 那么，这就是我等流派的大名了吗？
t_en: Then, this will be our school's official name?

t_ch: 就叫这个吧！
t_en: Let this be it!

t_ch: 让我再想想…
t_en: Give me another minute...

t_ch: 无妨，命名流派并非小事，阁下应当深思而后行。
t_en: Let us not be hasty. The naming of a school is a monumental task. My lord ought to be more prudent.

t_ch: 就叫「缭乱号飞艇」吧！
t_en: Let's call it the Dazzling Airship!

t_ch: 飞艇…诚然，我等音忍众的舞台应如忍•飞艇，令台下忍徒飘然若飞！
t_en: Airship... Indeed, our sound ninja's stage is akin to a ninja airship that elevates the audience to stratospheric heights!

t_ch: 那么，这就是我等流派的大名了吗？
t_en: Then, this will be our school's official name?

t_ch: 就叫这个吧！
t_en: Let this be it!

t_ch: 让我再想想…
t_en: Give me another minute...

t_ch: 无妨，命名流派并非小事，阁下应当深思而后行。
t_en: Let us not be hasty. The naming of a school is a monumental task. My lord ought to be more prudent.

t_ch: 就叫「缭乱忍徒旅店」吧！
t_en: Let's call it the Dazzling Initiate Hotel!

t_ch: 旅店…在下虽不解其中含义，但也觉得颇具意境。
t_en: Hotel... I do not fully grasp its meaning, but I reckon it evokes a rather artistic image.

t_ch: 那么，这就是我等流派的大名了吗？
t_en: Then, this will be our school's official name?

t_ch: 就叫这个吧！
t_en: Let this be it!

t_ch: 让我再想想…
t_en: Give me another minute...

t_ch: 无妨，命名流派并非小事，阁下应当深思而后行。
t_en: Let us not be hasty. The naming of a school is a monumental task. My lord ought to be more prudent.

t_ch: 就叫「缭乱流派对」吧！
t_en: Let's call it the Dazzling School Party!

t_ch: 派对…仅听名号就能知晓我等的音律将令忍徒们雀跃，也确实与折纸村当下的喧闹相称！
t_en: Party... Just hearing the name itself, one would know that we sound ninjas will send the other ninja into a rapture, and it matches the bustle of Paperfold Town!

t_ch: 那么，这就是我等流派的大名了吗？
t_en: Then, this will be our school's official name?

t_ch: 就叫这个吧！
t_en: Let this be it!

t_ch: 让我再想想…
t_en: Give me another minute...

t_ch: 无妨，命名流派并非小事，阁下应当深思而后行。
t_en: Let us not be hasty. The naming of a school is a monumental task. My lord ought to be more prudent.

t_ch: 很好，「{TEXTJOIN#120}」的音忍集合完毕！
t_en: Very well, {TEXTJOIN#120}'s sound ninja congregation is adjourned!

t_ch: 诸位若还有兴致，就请诸位随在下去勘察此次修行的「战场」吧。
t_en: If everyone is still interested, please follow me in the inspection of the training battleground.

t_ch: 是《银河忍法帖》中的乱破大人，第一次见到本人…还有盛会巨星同学！
t_en: Oh. Lady Rappa of the Cosmic Ninjutsu Inscriptions! It's my first time meeting you... and the Festive Superstar!

t_ch: 不对，现在流行的打招呼方式是——哟！{NICKNAME}•忍者阁下，缭乱•忍侠阁下！在下潮流•忍徒，参上！
t_en: No, wait, the trendy way to greet right now is... Yo! {NICKNAME} Ninja, Dazzling Ninja Hero! Trendy Ninja, reporting in!

t_ch: 不瞒你们，我早就听说乱破大人将在乐队中担任C位，于是自告奋勇接下了中央舞台的布置工作。有什么想知道的，尽管问我就好！
t_en: To be honest, I'd already heard that Lady Rappa would be leading the band, so I volunteered to accept the setup for the central stage. Just ask me if you wanna know anything!

t_ch: 话虽这么说…乐队要做的事其实很简单啦，在演出开场前完成想演奏的曲子就行。
t_en: I say that... but what the band has to do is actually pretty simple — to complete their song performance before the show starts proper.

t_ch: 帕姆！我的留声机呢？
t_en: Pom-Pom! Where's my phonograph?

t_ch: 好像…不能用。按校规，音乐派对的乐队…只能演奏自己创作的曲子。
t_en: It's probably... unusable. According to school regulations, the music party bands... must play their own original songs.

t_ch: 必须演奏原创的曲子？
t_en: They have to be original?

t_ch: 当然。要是随便用现成的乐曲就能完成演出，这场派对的水准也未免太低了。
t_en: Of course. If any existing piece could be used to complete the show, the quality of this party would be rock-bottom.

t_ch: 唔…嗯。嗯。此次作战的致胜之道，已然清晰浮现于在下眼前。
t_en: Hm.... Hm. Hm. The path to victory in this battle grows clearer before my very eyes.

t_ch: 登上演台，亦如步入战场！我等即是忍侠，观众即是至敌——是非成败系于弹指间。
t_en: Treading on the stage is akin to stepping onto the battlefield! I am a Ninja Hero, and the audience is the enemy — success or failure lies entirely betwixt our fingers.

t_ch: 为取得最佳战果，「{TEXTJOIN#120}」需做好充分准备。
t_en: To attain the most stellar victory, {TEXTJOIN#120} must make utmost preparations.

t_ch: 排练时间不多，把作曲的事定下来吧。
t_en: There's not much time left to practice. Let's tie down the composition.

t_ch: 按传统，音乐派对的演出曲目都是由谐乐学院决定。因此，我提议仍旧……
t_en: According to tradition, the music party's performance pieces are all decided by Charmony Academy. So, I propose that we still...

t_ch: 且慢！在下认为不妥。音忍要以钻研音律•忍法为己任，如此方可超越自身，不落凡俗。
t_en: Hold it! I don't think this is appropriate. A sound ninja must fervently observe the duty of studying the Sound Release ninjutsu, to transcend the self and rise above the mundane.

t_ch: 新创音律的忍道极为艰辛，忍首更应以身作则。故——编制音谱之责，就由在下完成！
t_en: The newly-formed way of the sound ninja's tenets may be arduous, which is why the Head Ninja must rise to the occasion. Therefore, the task of creating the composition has befallen me!

t_ch: 等等，你会作曲吗？
t_en: Hang on, do you know how to compose a song?

t_ch: 对呀，你会作曲吗？
t_en: Yeah, do you?

t_ch: 你还有什么惊喜是我不知道的？
t_en: What other surprises are you keeping from me?

t_ch: 哼哼哼，在下随苦茶大师修行数十载，遍历试炼万千，区区音律•忍法自然不在话下。
t_en: Heh-heh-heh, I trained with Master Kucha for ten years, and mastered countless trials. Such a meager Sound Release ninjutsu technique is naturally mere child's play.

t_ch: 只是…时隔久远，此番忍法无法尽数忆起。若能获得少许灵感，在下便能回忆起来。
t_en: It's just that... time has not been a kind mistress, and I struggle to recall that specific ninjutsu. My memory could be jogged if I obtain even the slightest inspiration.

t_ch: …你真的会吗？
t_en: ...Will you really?

t_ch: 没关系，我会！美丽的小姐，我来帮你。
t_en: It's okay, I will! Pretty lady, let me help.

t_ch: 主公！在下有要事委托。可否请你游历各地，<color=#dbc291ff>为在下收集谱曲用的灵感？</color>
t_en: Sound Master! I beseech you with a request: Could you <color=#dbc291ff>travel the worlds and collect music compositions for my inspiration?</color>

t_ch: 音律•忍法看似种类繁多，然奥义极为简单——「旋律乃是记录世界，绝非凭空编造。」
t_en: There may be different Sound Release ninjutsu styles, but our essence is very simple: "Rhythm is a record of the world, not conjured from thin air."

t_ch: 修行无处不在，望阁下在遍历过程中能有所感悟，以助力在下。
t_en: There are opportunities to train everywhere. I hope you will gain insight during this journey, to better aid me.

t_ch: 听着好复杂…
t_en: Sounds complex...

t_ch: 你确定这是我能听懂的吗？
t_en: You sure I'm going to understand this?

t_ch: 我想…忍首的意思是，音乐就在我们身边，希望你能到处看看，帮她收集作曲的灵感。应该…是这样吧？
t_en: I think... what the Chief Ninja means is that music is always around us, that you can take a look around and help her collect inspiration for compositions. That's... probably what she means?

t_ch: 嗯！不愧是与在下共同作战的忍众，阁下真是深得我心！
t_en: Yes! This is why you're a friendja. You're after my own heart!

t_ch: 谱曲之事就交由主公完成，至于诸位，请随在下返回道场，开启音律•修行。
t_en: Finding the compositions will be left to Sound Master, and as for everyone else, please follow me back to the training ground to commence sound training.

t_ch: 在下——定要将诸位训练成无惧任何演台的优秀音忍！
t_en: I... will definitely train everyone into outstanding ninjas who do not fear any stage!

t_ch: 作出决定后，乱破便先行去查看场地情况了……
t_en: After the decision was made, Rappa went off to check out the venue...

t_ch: ——盛会巨星！有件事想问！就是…我也可以加入你们嘛？
t_en: ...The Festive Superstar! I have something to ask! That is... can I join you all too?

t_ch: 刚才乱破大人的安排，我都听见啦。虽然我的资质不够，没法成为音忍，但这种通风报信…不对，是收集资讯的工作，我最擅长了！
t_en: I overheard everything about Lady Rappa's arrangements. I may not be adequately qualified to become a sound ninja, but if it's about gossip... I mean, I'm excellent at intel gathering work!

t_ch: 学生里谁和谁正在交往，老师里谁最近总挨学院代表的骂，要被裁员，我都一清二楚！
t_en: Who's dating who among the students, which teacher is being constantly berated by the academy's representative, and who's getting laid off, I've got all that info at my fingertips!

t_ch: 我可以帮你们到处打听，整理信息，标注位置…很方便的，让我加入吧！呜呜呜。
t_en: I can ask around for you, organize the information, label their locations... It'll be super convenient for you! Please let me join! Boohoo!

t_ch: 我同意了。
t_en: Yes, I'll do it.

t_ch: 对不起，我喜欢自己来。
t_en: Sorry, I prefer to do it myself.

t_ch: 你是认真的？你确定要拒绝这位热情而乐于助人的同学，选择自己跑遍整个银河？
t_en: Are you serious? You sure you want to reject a passionate, helpful student, and choose to run yourself ragged all over the cosmos?

t_ch: 要知道，即便这里是学校，也不是每个角落都能碰到乐队，随便抓个学生都是吉他英雄的。灵感这东西…可遇而不可求啊。
t_en: You must know that though this is a school, it's not like you're going to bump into bands along every hallway — not every student you accost is going to be some guitarist hero. Such a thing like inspiration... it comes when you least expect it.

t_ch: 我同意了。
t_en: Yes, I'll do it.

t_ch: 好耶！我现在就去群里发消息，等等啊，很快就会有回复了。
t_en: Awesome! I'll post a message in the group now. Just give it a moment. Someone will reply real quick.

t_ch: 你看着蒙塔娜的手指以极快的速度在屏幕上敲击起来，终于明白了为何原始博士没有选择将人类变为其他动物——有手实在太重要了。
t_en: You watch as Montana's fingers rapidly tap on the screen, finally realizing why Dr. Primitive didn't choose to turn humans into other animals — fingers are really handy.

t_ch: 来了来了！群友的智慧果然无穷无尽。都给你整理好了，看看吧！
t_en: Got it! Can't beat the collective intellect of group chat. I've got it all here for you. Take a look!

t_ch: 你们觉得这个领队靠谱吗？
t_en: You sure this leader is reliable?

t_ch: 有趣的小姐。充满迷人的魅力，我从未见过如此特别的学生。
t_en: A very interested young lady filled with charm. I've never met such a special student.

t_ch: 她很自信的样子。应该没问题…吧？
t_en: She looks confident. There shouldn't be a problem... should there?

t_ch: 至少比我强……
t_en: At least she's better than me...

t_ch: 我就不该问你们。无论如何，距离首场演出没多少时间，先完成学院的任务再说吧。
t_en: I never should've asked you all. There's not much time left before the first show. We'll talk about this after finishing the school's task.

t_ch: 希望她在排练时候能稍微正经点。
t_en: I hope she'll be a little more serious during practice.

t_ch: ——此番演奏是何等的忍•神速！实在令人目不暇接。然而…还能更快！
t_en: ...A performance that is truly worthy of ninja-like swiftness! Absolutely overwhelming! However... it can be even faster!

t_ch: 噢！主公，你成功归来了！快看看在下的*缭乱•极•特训*成果吧！
t_en: Oh! Master, you've successfully returned! Quickly, come take a look at the fruits of the Dazzling's special extreme training!

t_ch: 先说清楚，打扮成这样是演出需要，我只是勉强配合你们。
t_en: Don't get the wrong idea or anything. I'm only dressed like this because it's part of the show. I'll cooperate, but only to a certain extent.

t_ch: 我觉得挺好，玩乐队就要有个亮眼的样子！乱破小姐，你的设计真是太棒了！
t_en: I think it's fine. A band needs to look snappy! Ms. Rappa, your design is most excellent!

t_ch: 乱破同学改造过的贝斯…用起来也很顺手。
t_en: The bass tweaked by Rappa... it's working smoothly.

t_ch: 主公的看法如何？是不是颇有「缭乱流」音忍的气势了？
t_en: What does Sound Master think? Does it have the vibe of a Dazzling Sound Ninja?

t_ch: 玩COSPLAY不带我是吧？
t_en: Are you excluding me from the cosplay party?

t_ch: 此乃音忍的特制装束——「缭乱•忍法•缤纷万象武装」是也！
t_en: This is a sound ninja's tailor-made attire — the Vibrant Dazzling Ninjutsu Warsuit!

t_ch: 若是未经在下的严格特训，即便是主公，也难以驾驭这般忍具。
t_en: One, including you, would be hard-pressed to master such a ningu without undergoing rigorous training.

t_ch: 这就是我最想看的！
t_en: This is what I most wanted to see!

t_ch: 多谢！此乃音忍的特制装束——「缭乱•忍法•缤纷万象武装」！
t_en: Thank you! This is a sound ninja's tailor-made attire — the Vibrant Dazzling Ninjutsu Warsuit!

t_ch: 登场可震慑对手心魄，行动间亦能吸引目光，实乃演台作战必备的专门•忍具是也！
t_en: Striking fear into opponents' hearts the moment you step on to the stage, your slightest movement capturing the attention of all — such a specialized ningu is essential for the battle performance!

t_ch: 就算是为了增强舞台效果，为什么要把脸遮住？
t_en: Even if it's to boost our stage presence, why must we cover our faces?

t_ch: 阁下，村中看似平静，实则暗潮涌动，贼人勾心斗角，切不可放松警惕，疏于提防。
t_en: The town may seem serene, but dark tides threaten to surge forth. Scoundrels bicker among themselves, and we mustn't lower our guard, oblivious to the surrounding dangers.

t_ch: …你说是就是吧。时间不多，你快点把曲子写好，要准备合奏了。
t_en: ...Whatever you say. There's not much time left. Hurry up and finish up the composition. We have to start playing.

t_ch: 你将自己的奇特经历详细告诉了乱破……
t_en: You tell Rappa about your strange experiences in detail...

t_ch: 在下果然没看错。主公的音忍•资质远在常人之上。
t_en: My judgment was sound about you. Your sound ninja aptitude is beyond average.

t_ch: 仅靠观察音律现象，便能从中得到如此丰富的灵感，实在是…高明！
t_en: To be able to glean such a wealth of inspiration from mere observation of musical phenomena, you are truly... ingenious!

t_ch: 唔。这就是苦茶大师说过的「开悟」吗？在下必将尽心谱曲，不负主公的万般努力。
t_en: Hmm. Is this the "enlightenment" of which Master Kucha spoke? I must devote myself to composing, ensuring that I do not squander Sound Master's utmost efforts.

t_ch: 原来你真会作曲啊？
t_en: So you really know how to compose then?

t_ch: 与主公相识的时日已经不短了，在下何时骗过你？
t_en: We have been acquainted with each other for long. When have I ever been dishonest?

t_ch: 即便信奉的忍道有所差别，言出必行乃是在下闯荡忍界的信条，未曾有任何懈怠。
t_en: The ninja tenets we pursue may differ, but my creed in the ninja world has always been to keep my word — I have never reneged in this regard.

t_ch: 需要我帮忙作词吗？
t_en: Do you need my help to write the lyrics?

t_ch: 此次作战用到的音律•忍法乃是「无念白•忍•电音轮舞」，就不劳烦主公编撰真言了。
t_en: The Sound Release ninjutsu employed in this battle is the Wordless White Ninja String Vibration Wheel Dance. I would not trouble you for the wordsmithing.

t_ch: 哼…嗯…嗯。
t_en: Hmph... Hmm... Mm.

t_ch: 糟糕！光顾着和主公聊天，方才回忆起的曲调都忘得精光了！
t_en: Disaster! I have been so absorbed in our discourse that I've completely forgotten the tune I recalled!

t_ch: 不妙不妙。在下这就开始闭关，潜心创作。若是主公有空，不妨去与诸位音忍聊聊。
t_en: This is bad, very bad. I will enter recluse and immerse myself in creation. If you aren't busy, you might have a chat with the other sound ninja.

t_ch: 容在下先静待片刻——
t_en: Please allow me a moment of silence...

t_ch: 哼哼哼哼哼哼——哼哼哈嘿！
t_en: Hhhnnnggghh-hnngh-hnngh-haahey!

t_ch: （乱破确实在很认真地构思乐曲，还是别打扰她了。）
t_en: (Rappa really is churning her creative juices. I'd better not bother her.)

t_ch: 哼…哼…哼…哼…哼…哼哼哼——
t_en: Hnngh... Hnngh... Hnngh... Hnngh... Hnngh...Hnngghh...

t_ch: （乱破确实在很认真地构思乐曲，还是别打扰她了。）
t_en: (Rappa really is churning her creative juices. I'd better not bother her.)

t_ch: 哼…哼…哼…哼……哼哼——哼哼哼哼哼…哼哼…哼！
t_en: Hnngh... Hnngh... Hnngh... Hnngghh... Hhhnngh-hnngh-hnngh-hnngh...Hnngghh... Hnngh!

t_ch: （乱破确实在很认真地构思乐曲，还是别打扰她了。）
t_en: (Rappa really is churning her creative juices. I'd better not bother her.)

t_ch: ——主公！
t_en: ...Sound Master!

t_ch: 修行反复被打断，即便是在下这般优秀的忍者也实在难以忍受！还请离开此地！失礼！
t_en: My meditation is being interrupted. Even an outstanding ninja such as myself cannot withstand such disruption! I ask for leave from this place! Excuse me!

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}，有件事让我很担忧。
t_en: {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} Manager, I'm very concerned about something.

t_ch: 这位乐队核心，获得学院认可的这位乱破同学…真的懂音乐吗？
t_en: This Rappa who's supposed to be the band leader, and was even acknowledged by the academy... Does she really know music?

t_ch: 或许吧。
t_en: Perhaps.

t_ch: 不好说。
t_en: It's hard to say.

t_ch: 都让她懂完了。
t_en: She's SSR grade in music.

t_ch: 她连话都说不明白，而且缺乏基本的协作能力与团队意识。如果没有能力还继续担任核心，那演出的风险一定会很大…要是在台上出丑，会影响我们的前途。
t_en: Her words hardly even make much sense, and she lacks basic cooperative capability and understanding of teamwork. If she's unqualified but continues to bear the heaviest responsibility, this performance runs a huge risk... Our futures will be affected if we flop on stage.

t_ch: 你要相信队友。
t_en: You must trust your bandmates.

t_ch: 那要不还是取消吧。
t_en: How about we cancel the show then?

t_ch: 演出已经开始宣传了，现在说这话已经来不及了。
t_en: The show's promotion has already started. It's too late to say that now.

t_ch: 风险越大，惊喜越大！
t_en: The greater the risk, the bigger the surprise!

t_ch: …算了，出于对您与学院的信任，我会等首场演出结束后再做判断…您不要抱有什么期待就好。
t_en: ...Forget it. I'll trust in you and the academy, and wait till the first show ends to pass judgment... Just don't expect too much out of it.

t_ch: 既然经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}都清楚，为什么不提出反对？
t_en: Since {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} Manager knows about this, why didn't you raise any objections?

t_ch: 要是事先知道我就会阻止了…
t_en: I would have if I knew about it beforehand...

t_ch: 讨论都结束了，乱破却突然出现，对不起没能阻止她…你一定很生气吧？
t_en: The discussion was already over, and Rappa suddenly appeared. I'm sorry I couldn't stop her... You must be very upset?

t_ch: 你这个问题问得很好。
t_en: That's a very good question.

t_ch: 就是…我们团队的情况你也清楚，事情走到这步，谁都没办法对吧。
t_en: Mm... you're clear on our band's situation. I guess there's no right party when the matter has gotten this out of hand.

t_ch: …明白了。出于对学院的信任，我会等首场演出结束后再做判断。请您不要抱有期待。
t_en: ...Understood. I'll trust in you and the academy, and wait till the first show ends to pass judgment. Just don't expect too much out of it.

t_ch: 出于对您与学院的信任，我会等首场演出结束后再做判断…您不要抱有什么期待就好。
t_en: I'll trust in you and the academy, and wait till the first show ends to pass judgment... Just don't expect too much out of it.

t_ch: 贝斯好难啊，我怎么都弹不好……
t_en: The bass is so hard. Why can't I get it right...

t_ch: 没关系，能出声就够了。
t_en: It's okay. It's fine as long as you can make sounds.
